questions.question: Briefly state the purpose of the forget gate in an LSTM.
expectedPoints: It decides which information from the previous cell state to discard.
It outputs a value between 0 (completely forget) and 1 (completely remember) for each element in the cell state.
It helps the network eliminate irrelevant information from past steps.

questions.question: Explain in detail how the architecture of an LSTM network addresses the vanishing gradient problem that plagues standard RNNs. Use the relevant equations in your explanation.
expectedPoints: Describe the vanishing gradient problem in simple RNNs (multiplication of many derivatives < 1).
Introduce the LSTM's cell state (C_t) as the key component.
Write the cell state update equation: C_t = f_t * C_{t-1} + i_t * ~C_t.
Explain that this is an additive (not multiplicative) operation.
Argue that the gradient ∂C_t/∂C_{t-1} is approximately f_t (a learned value), allowing the gradient to flow back through many steps without vanishing exponentially.
Mention the role of the gates in regulating this flow of information.
